cards.title: Water Enema
cards._legacyHtml:
Types:
  • Cleansing enema: Warm water (37–38°C), 500–1000 mL. For constipation and pre-treatment bowel prep.
  • Retention enema: 100–250 mL held 15–20 min. For local mucosal effect.
Equipment: Enema can/bag, rubber tube, rectal nozzle, stand, lubricant (coconut oil), gloves.
Procedure: Left lateral position → lubricate nozzle → insert 8–10 cm → release clamp slowly → hold 5–10 min → expel.
Frequency: 1–2× daily for detox protocols; 1× weekly maintenance.
Water temperature: 37°C (body temp) — never hot.

cards.title: Coffee Enema
cards._legacyHtml:
Preparation: 3 tbsp organic ground coffee + 1L water. Boil 3 min, simmer 15 min, strain, cool to 37°C.
Volume: 500 mL per session. Retain for 12–15 minutes.
Mechanism: Palmitates in coffee stimulate glutathione S-transferase in liver → enhanced Phase II detox. Theophylline dilates bile ducts → bile flow ↑.
Indications: Liver detox protocols, chronic fatigue, cancer supportive (Gerson therapy), constipation, headaches from toxin load.
Frequency: 1–3× weekly (detox); once daily during Gerson protocol.
Contraindications: Caffeine sensitivity, IBS, ulcerative colitis, cardiac arrhythmias.

cards.title: Herbal Enema
cards._legacyHtml:
Common Herbs Used:
  • Triphala decoction (100 mL): Anti-inflammatory, colon tonic — IBS, IBD, constipation.
  • Dashamoola decoction (100 mL): Vata pacifying — neurological, musculoskeletal conditions (Ayurvedic Basti).
  • Chamomile (250 mL): Antispasmodic — spastic colon, anxiety, insomnia.
  • Neem decoction (100 mL): Antimicrobial — parasites, colon infections.
Preparation: 20 g herb per 500 mL water, decoct to 250 mL, strain, cool to body temp. Administer as retention enema 10–15 min.

cards.title: Oil Enema
cards._legacyHtml:
Oils Used: Sesame oil (warm), olive oil, coconut oil. Volume: 100–200 mL.
Mechanism: Lubricates and softens hardened faecal matter; soothes intestinal mucosa; helps impacted stool release.
Indications: Faecal impaction, chronic constipation, spastic colon, pre-operative bowel prep, anal fissure (soothing).
Procedure: Warm oil to 37–38°C. Administer 100–200 mL. Retain 30–60 minutes (or overnight if tolerated). Follow with cleansing water enema next morning.
Ayurvedic use: Snehavasti (Basti with oil) — pacifies Vata, nourishes colon and nervous system.

cards.title: Colonic Irrigation (Colonic Hydrotherapy)
cards._legacyHtml:
Definition: Controlled infusion and evacuation of warm water through the entire large bowel via closed-circuit machine.
Volume: 20–60 litres of water used per session (cycled in and out). Water temp: 37–38°C. Pressure: ≤0.3 bar.
Duration: 30–45 min per session. Course: 3–6 sessions over 2–3 weeks.
Indications: Constipation, IBS, pre-colonoscopy prep, detox, candidiasis, colon cleansing protocols.
Equipment: FDA-registered colonic hydrotherapy device; disposable speculum and tubing.
Advantage over enema: Reaches entire colon including transverse and ascending sections.

cards.title: Standard Detox Enema Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
7-Day Enema Detox Protocol (BNYS):
Days 1–2: Plain water enema, 500 mL, morning, 37°C — bowel preparation and irrigation.
Days 3–4: Triphala herbal enema, 250 mL retention, 12 min — colon toning and microbiome support.
Days 5–6: Coffee enema, 500 mL retention, 12 min — liver and biliary detox phase.
Day 7: Oil enema (sesame), 150 mL retention, 30 min — mucosal nourishment and closure.
Post-protocol: Probiotics (Lactobacillus + Bifidobacterium 10 billion CFU/day) for 2 weeks. Maintain high-fibre diet.

cards.title: Contraindications — Colonic Therapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Absolute Contraindications:
  • Acute abdomen (appendicitis, peritonitis)
  • Rectal or colon cancer (active)
  • Rectal fissures or perforations
  • Recent bowel surgery (<6 weeks)
  • Severe haemorrhoids (Grade III–IV)
  • Uncontrolled inflammatory bowel disease (acute flare)
  • Pregnancy
  • Congestive heart failure
  • Severe electrolyte imbalance
Relative Contraindications:
  • Cirrhosis / severe liver disease
  • Renal failure
  • Hernia (large inguinal/abdominal)
  • Diverticulitis (acute)

cards.title: Equipment & Hygiene Standards
cards._legacyHtml:
Basic Enema Set:
  • Enema can/bag (2 L capacity)
  • Soft rubber/silicone tubing with flow clamp
  • Rectal nozzle (lubricated tip)
  • IV stand or hook (height: 45–60 cm above rectum for gravity flow)
  • Waterproof mat, absorbent pads
  • Disposable gloves, lubricant (coconut/KY jelly)
Hygiene: All equipment sterilised between patients (autoclave or 2% glutaraldehyde). Single-use nozzles preferred. Colonic machines: use disposable speculums and lines. Document lot numbers and disinfection log.
